live_path.role: CLI entrypoint parses --owner and forwards it to the service layer.
Orchestrates owner resolution, base-record creation, routing-key derivation, and payload emission.
Derives effective owner and owner_source from explicit --owner or config defaults.
Builds the persisted base record using the already-resolved owner.
Creates the base record with name, status, and owner only.
Computes routing_key from the resolved effective owner and item name.
Formats routing_key from owner and name after slugification.
Emits final payload by attaching owner_source and routing_key to the base record.
Adds owner_source and routing_key to a copy of the record for emission.

owner_source.rule: Returns "explicit" when a non-empty owner argument is supplied; otherwise returns "default" with config/defaults.json owner.

not_derived_from.reason: Base record contains only name, status, and owner.
Helper reads a record field but is not used by the live sync path.

routing_key.rule: Slugifies owner and name, then formats `<owner>:<name>`.
routing_key.timing: Computed after CLI owner forwarding and after owner resolution, before serialize_payload emission.

emission.rule: Final payload is a copy of the base record plus owner_source and routing_key.

test_observations.observation: Explicit owner `pm-oncall` yields owner_source `explicit` and routing_key `pm-oncall:launch-checklist`.
Missing --owner falls back to config/defaults.json owner and emits owner_source `default`.
CLI path preserves explicit owner through to emitted owner_source and routing_key.
Repo docs intentionally reference --owner, owner_source, routing_key, and sync_item.

rejected_decoys.reason: Suggests owner_source could be inferred from a stored record, but it is unused in the live path.
Legacy helper adds owner_source in storage, but sync_item does not call it.
Legacy routing helper is unused; live code imports and calls sync_app.serializer.build_routing_key.
Narrative speculation conflicts with the implemented call path and tests.
